name: Arachnoid_Mech

infobox.Systems: Arachnoid Injector(Ranged)EffectDamage target, creating a friendly robot spider on kill.(1 damage)Upgrade I+1 Damage ()Increases the artillery attack damage by 1.Upgrade IIAcidic ()Adds Acid to the attack of the Arachnoid.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theArachnophilessquad.
sections.Strategy:
Compared to other Ranged mechs such theArtillery Mech, the Arachnoid Mech does not have an immediate push with its default weapon. Instead, the Arachnoid Injector is a low-damage weapon that hits a single target, and demands killing an enemy to spawn an Arachnoid unit; this friendly spider-like unit can then be used to push another target. Functionally, the spawned Arachnoid can be seen as a very flexible push that can be banked for later turns if it isn't needed immediately.
The spawned Arachnoid unit has 1 health, 3 movement, and a melee attack which does 1 damage, pushes away, and kills the Arachnoid. Unlike theBombling Mech's bombs, Arachnoids only dismantle after attacking and not at the end of the turn. Unless the player has a clear advantage, using them to block spawns should be avoided if there are more than 2 turns remaining, as their value tends to be higher than a single spawn block. Other effective uses of Arachnoids include blocking movement of grounded enemies, using them to bait enemies into attacking them, moving them to collect time pods, or enabling a ricochet from theBulk Mechin the Arachnophiles squad.
The health, movement and damage for the friendly Arachnoid units is not affected by upgrades to the Arachnoid Mech itself; but the Arachnoid can gain A.C.I.D. to its attack if theAcidicupgrade is powered for the Arachnoid Injector weapon. An Arachnoid that spawns from an enemy inflicted with A.C.I.D will absorb theA.C.I.D. Pool; when the same Arachnoid self-destructs after attacking, it will similarly leave an A.C.I.D. pool in that tile. However, beware of any other environmental effects when killing enemies, such as cracking a tile with the damage, killing flying enemies over chasms and water, or enemies exploding when defeated under theBlast Psion's explosive decay; under these conditions, the Arachnoid will instantly be destroyed after spawning.
Going for either the Acidic or+1 damageupgrade first is preference dependent, though both should be considered high-priority upgrades as they both help the Arachnoid Mech's lackluster damage output. Being able to secure kills – against Psions in particular with increased damage – is critical for this Mech to function. Pilots withBoost, such asKai MillerorMorgan Lejeune(or anyone with the Opener skill) can help score kills too. Look to pick up a more conventional Ranged weapon with immediate push utility during the run, as the added flexibility can be useful when enemies get higher HP and can't be taken out on the first turn.
If using the Arachnoid Mech under aCustom Squad, it is recommended to pair it with Mechs that can soften enemies for it to finish them off. Additionally, theBoulder Mechhas unique synergy with it, as it is possible to also spawn Arachnoids from boulders; this can also alleviateRock Accelerator's2-directional limited push, by letting the Arachnoid Mech target the leftover boulder and use the Arachnoid to push another nearby target.
